roadmap.goal: Add AsyncStorage write-through durability to Firestore trip adapter so native cold-start offline does not lose trip data. Scope: trip adapter only.

validation.rationale: Bugfix scope. RCA approved by user. 2-step minimal roadmap.

phases.name: Trip adapter offline durability
phases.description: Regression test then AsyncStorage write-through fix.

steps.name: Regression test trip offline cold-start
steps.scenario_name: Trip adapter hydrates from AsyncStorage offline
criteria: Test uses AsyncStorage mock and fake Firestore with exists-false snapshot.
Test asserts fresh adapter loads trip written by prior adapter same uid.
Test asserts carryover saved on adapter A is visible on adapter B.
Test fails against current code proving the bug.
steps.implementation_notes: Use jest with @react-native-async-storage/async-storage/jest/async-storage-mock. Fake Firestore as minimal object exposing doc() and onSnapshot() that fires {exists: ()=>false, data: ()=>undefined}. Functional style: pure factory setup, no class fixtures.

steps.name: Fix AsyncStorage write-through trip adapter
steps.scenario_name: Trip adapter hydrates from AsyncStorage offline
criteria: All existing tests still pass; firestore-trip-storage.test.ts has no regressions.
firestore-trip-storage mirrors trip and carryover to AsyncStorage on every write.
initialize hydrates caches from AsyncStorage before onSnapshot and does not clobber with empty snapshot.
useAppInitialization stale comments corrected firebase-config gains explanatory comment.
steps.implementation_notes: Keys: firestore-cache:v1:{uid}:trip and firestore-cache:v1:{uid}:carryover. In initialize() Promise.all read both keys; if data present, set caches and flip isTripInitialized=true so first empty onSnapshot doesn't clobber. Writes remain fire-and-forget. Keep functional factory style. Do NOT add persistentLocalCache() on native (crashes per firebase-js-sdk#7947). Pending-write queue deferred.
